notes: Each jump requires 2 clicks: select piece (click on GREEN=14 center), then click arrow (GRAY=2 hollow square)
Arrow appears as hollow 4x4 square of GRAY(2) pixels at jump destination
Selection shows as DARK_GRAY(3) border around clicked piece
Piece sprite name contains 'fozwvlovdui', arrow sprite name contains 'lgbyiaitpdi'
Arrow offset from piece = (dx*12, dy*12) where dx,dy is jump direction
Grid string: row2='.xx.x..', row3='.....x.', row5='    .x.' (x=piece, .=empty cell)
After jump, click coordinates for next select often equal arrow coordinates (piece moved there)

key_findings.click_detection: rozmjeilxc(x,y) checks sprite bounds with camera transform
key_findings.piece_handler: dghsidbuet(x,y) dispatches: fozwvlovdui=piece click, lgbyiaitpdi=arrow click
key_findings.piece_click_handler: xpcuvjyrgu: shows arrows at valid jump destinations
key_findings.arrow_click_handler: cfilhtifcb: executes jump (moves piece, removes captured piece)
key_findings.arrow_positioning: lgbyiaitpdi.cdpcbbnfdp = (dx*2*6, dy*2*6) relative to selected piece
key_findings.win_check_positions: L1 win at fldpqdkmge in [(0,2), (2,2), (5,1)] — not all positions win

colors.pieces: GREEN(14) 4x4 diamond pattern on OFF_WHITE(1) 4x4 cell
colors.arrows: GRAY(2) hollow 4x4 square at jump destination
